source.title: Temu Watch: Logistics in times of tariffs + multiple sources on Temu GMV

extractions.claim: Temu's 2025 GMV was approximately $92.5B globally, up ~70-75% from 2024
extractions.evidence: Temu's annual sales amounted to US$92,514m (~$92.5 billion) in 2025. Temu's GMV in 2024 was ~$70.8B. LatePost reported Temu's full-year GMV target for 2025 was US$100 billion. ECDB data shows Temu as part of Pinduoduo's combined $817B GMV for 2025.

verification.verifier_notes: Multiple sources converge on $90-95B range for Temu 2025. LatePost target of $100B suggests slight underperformance due to tariff disruptions in Q2.

extractions.claim: Temu H1 2025 GMV was ~$35B, implying H2 was ~$57B (heavy back-weighting due to tariff recovery)
extractions.evidence: Temu achieved a global GMV of approximately $35 billion in the first half of 2025. Full-year ~$92.5B implies H2 was ~$57.5B. Q3 projected at $22-25B.

verification.verifier_notes: H1 weakness consistent with U.S. tariff disruption in May 2025 (daily users fell 48%, sales dropped 23%). H2 recovery from tariff rate reduction and EU growth.

extractions.claim: US tariff impact on Temu was severe but temporary: 23% sales drop, then recovery after tariff reduction to ~54%
extractions.evidence: US de minimis removal caused 23% sales drop. Daily U.S. users fell 48% in May vs March 2025. Temu suspended U.S. marketing on May 1. Tariff on fully managed deliveries reduced from 120% to ~54%. By H2, GMV composition: US 31%, Europe 40%.

verification.verifier_notes: From TechBuzzChina, ChinaTalk, and Modern Retail reporting on Temu's tariff response

extractions.claim: Temu 2026 GMV forecast: ~$120-130B with 30-40% growth, but EU de minimis removal is a risk
extractions.evidence: Growth target for 2026 is 30% (company internal). Analyst estimates suggest 35-40% growth. $92.5B x 1.35 = $125B. EU plans to remove 150 EUR threshold in 2026 (moved forward from 2028). Temu expanding semi-managed model and German warehouse hub in preparation.

verification.verifier_notes: 30% is internal target per LatePost. EU regulatory risk could clip growth to 25-30% if enacted mid-2026.
